findings.title: DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 (clone-depth audit) completed: PROP-049-clone-depth-standardization authored.
findings.details: Performed comprehensive clone-create site audit per directive tasks 1-4. Exhaustive grep across monitor/prompts/, monitor/prompts/reference/, monitor/scripts/, build-scripts/ for 'git clone' literal. Found 8 total prompt-side sites. 7 of 8 already have --depth 50 on GitHub HEAD: 4 from operator-direct 2026-05-18 (curmudgeon.md, workspace-sync.md L25, decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md — these are STALE in FUSE; see FIND-02) + 3 since-creation per PROPs 038/039/041 (curmudgeon-verify, sloppytoppy-score, sloppytoppy-rewrite). 1 remaining un-patched site is workspace-sync.md L581 (strand-rescue recipe inside JSON template). Plus the baby-clone mystery: analyst.md Step 0a and analyst-baby.md Step 0a only REFRESH existing clones — never create — yet baby's 2026-05-18T05:14Z run reported a 659MB clone existed. Investigated 3 hypotheses (harness pre-clones / session reuse / vestigial code from prior version); H1 (harness) is most likely. PROP recommends 4 patches: P1 fixes the strand-rescue line, P2 adds a defensive clone-create with --depth 50 to analyst.md Step 0a's else-branch (closes the implicit-clone mystery), P3 updates analyst-baby.md inheritance reference, P4 documents --depth 50 as a CLAUDE.md convention.
findings.recommended_action: Operator: review PROP-049. Apply via fresh clone (NOT FUSE — would revert the 2026-05-18 patches). Optionally apply P1 alone (4-line surgical) and defer P2/P3/P4 pending harness investigation (operator escape hatch documented in counter_arg Q3). Following apply, run `node build.js publish` to sync FUSE back to canonical state (resolves the 4-file staleness too).
findings.evidence: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-049-clone-depth-standardization.json (4 patches, 6 counter-arguments, 6 regression-checks, 4 out-of-scope followups). DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 marked status=completed in FUSE (status + completed_at + completed_by_run + prop_id_authored — DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit).

findings.title: 5 monitor/prompts/ files STALE in FUSE — operator-direct 2026-05-18 edits never round-tripped
findings.details: Four of the five operator-direct patches from 2026-05-18 (curmudgeon.md L52, workspace-sync.md L25, decider-patches-and-selfapply.md L71, OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md L14) added --depth 50 to clone commands on GitHub but never propagated back to the FUSE workspace. 46+ hours later FUSE still has the un-patched versions. This is the same direction-asymmetry that PROP-045 (workspace-sync anti-reversion guard) addressed and that DIRECTIVE-20260519-001 + DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 both got stuck on at the directive-discovery layer. The structural fix would be either (a) periodic build.js publish on a scheduled task, (b) workspace-sync gains a git→FUSE pull for OWNED_BY_GIT files. Neither is implemented. The agents are unaffected because they clone fresh from GitHub HEAD, but tinker reading FUSE sees stale prose and any operator file-tools edit would revert the 2026-05-18 patches.
findings.recommended_action: After applying PROP-049, run `node build.js publish` to force git→FUSE sync — resolves all 5 stale files in one stroke. Sibling PROP-050 (or PROP-051) for the structural fix is recommended (PROP-049 FU3 references this).
findings.evidence: Per-file md5 comparison this run: 4 of 7 spot-checked prompt files STALE; 7 of 8 audit-target files in PROP-049 STALE.

findings.title: build-scripts/generate-html.js: 1-line caption diff persists 4th consecutive run.
findings.details: Workspace has caption 'plus 1 analyst-added claim (WIN-070)'; GitHub does not. Cross-checked data/wins.json — there is no analyst_added field and no 'WIN-070' key (wins use bare numeric ids; '070' exists but no analyst-added marker). The workspace caption math (69 + 1 + 2 = 72) matches the actual catalog total (72 wins), while the GitHub-rendered docs/index.html caption (69 + 2 = 71) is OFF BY ONE — a genuine integrity issue in published HTML. Either: (a) docs/index.html needs a fix to add the +1 analyst-added or accept the math discrepancy, or (b) build-scripts/generate-html.js workspace version needs to revert. Recommend integrity agent flag this on next 9 AM run.
findings.recommended_action: Defer to integrity agent or next Mode 3 audit. Now a documented integrity finding, not just FUSE noise.

findings.title: EXP-425 / ISS-2105 (86 missing EXP IDs in expansion-tracker audit) — routed to tinker via expansion-tracker but not via operator-directives.
findings.details: Discovered during pre-flight backlog-metrics computation: expansion-tracker.json has EXP-425 with assigned_to='tinker', source='decider-bau-triage', status='pending', routed_from_iss='ISS-2105'. ISS-2105 is severity=major in open-issues.json. This is a legitimate tinker work item that came in via decider's BAU triage path rather than via operator-directives — a routing path the dispatcher pre-flight does NOT scan. The tinker dispatcher only scans operator-directives/, not expansion-tracker.json filtered by assigned_to='tinker'. Two questions: (1) should the dispatcher also scan expansion-tracker for tinker-assigned items? (2) should EXP-425 be done in a future tinker run? Deferred to next Mode 3 / Mode 4 run.
findings.recommended_action: Operator decision: extend dispatcher pre-flight to scan expansion-tracker for assigned_to='tinker' items, OR re-route EXP-425 / ISS-2105 to operator-directives so the existing dispatcher path picks it up. Either way, EXP-425 itself (the 86-EXP-ID gap audit) is genuine work that should be scheduled.
